title: Cross-Platform Portability
requirements:
Scripts work on Windows (MSYS/Git Bash), macOS, and Linux without modification.

1. Audit and fix grep usage:
   - Replace all grep -P with grep -E across all scripts
   - Verify no grep flags incompatible with macOS default grep
   - Update REPAIR_KNOWLEDGE.json: promote repair-1 to 'high' confidence, mark as applied

2. Fix hardcoded paths:
   - Replace hardcoded Windows gitleaks path in run-secret-scan.sh with: GITLEAKS=$(command -v gitleaks 2>/dev/null || which gitleaks 2>/dev/null)
   - Replace any hardcoded path separators (backslash) with forward slash
   - Audit all scripts for C:\ or Windows-specific paths

3. Fix bash version compatibility:
   - Audit all scripts for bash 4+ features (associative arrays declare -A, mapfile, readarray, ${var,,} lowercase)
   - Replace with POSIX-compatible alternatives where possible
   - Where bash 4+ is required: add version check at script top

4. Fix date command portability:
   - Audit all scripts using date: macOS uses BSD date, Linux uses GNU date
   - Replace date -d (GNU) with portable alternative
   - Test timestamp generation on both platforms

5. Add platform detection to scripts/output.sh:
   - Detect PLATFORM via uname -s
   - Set IS_WINDOWS, IS_MACOS, IS_LINUX booleans
   - Scripts that need platform-specific behavior source this file

6. Write tests:
   - Run all scripts through shellcheck (static analysis for portability)
   - Test gitleaks path resolution with gitleaks in PATH vs not
   - Test timestamp generation matches ISO 8601 format
   - Verify no grep -P remains in any script
   - Verify no hardcoded Windows paths remain
acceptance_criteria: No grep -P usage remains in any script (all replaced with grep -E)
No hardcoded Windows paths in any script
run-secret-scan.sh uses dynamic gitleaks path resolution
All bash 4+ features have POSIX alternatives or version checks
date commands are portable across macOS BSD date and GNU date
scripts/output.sh includes platform detection (IS_WINDOWS, IS_MACOS, IS_LINUX)
REPAIR_KNOWLEDGE.json repair-1 promoted to high confidence
